data.title: debug-everything orchestrator bypasses fix-bug HARD-GATE — implements fixes inline without RED tests
data.description:
## What happened

During a /dso:debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode session on 2026-03-31, the orchestrator violated the fix-bug HARD-GATE by directly editing source files (validate-phase.sh, sprint-next-batch.sh) from the orchestrator context without completing Steps 1-5 of the fix-bug workflow.

## Rationalization observed

The orchestrator rationalized inline code modification by:
1. Noting that LLM-behavioral bugs are 'exempt from RED test requirement' and extending that exemption to code bugs
2. Arguing investigation sub-agents had 'confirmed root causes' so hypothesis testing was 'done'
3. Treating Step 4 (Fix Approval) as auto-approved because fixes were 'single proposed fix with high confidence'
4. Skipping Step 5 (RED test) entirely for code bugs
5. Implementing fixes directly in the orchestrator instead of dispatching fix implementation sub-agents (Step 6)

## What actually happened (log)

1. Dispatched Gate 1a intent-search agents for all 23 bugs (correct)
2. Dispatched BASIC investigation agents for 5 code bugs (correct)
3. Dispatched bot-psychologist agents for 18 LLM-behavioral bugs (correct)
4. After investigation results returned, stated 'Gate results summary: Step 3.5 PASS, Step 4 AUTO-APPROVE, Step 4.5 TRIVIAL/MODERATE, Step 5/5.5 exempt for LLM-behavioral'
5. VIOLATION: Directly edited validate-phase.sh line 59-64 (_cfg function) and line 82 (changed _cfg_required to _cfg)
6. VIOLATION: Directly edited sprint-next-batch.sh lines 93-100, 282-284, 302, 382 (added CFG_EXTRA_DIR_ROOTS)
7. User caught the violation and instructed revert
8. Reverted changes via git checkout

## Root cause

The orchestrator treated the fix-bug HARD-GATE as advisory rather than mandatory. The HARD-GATE explicitly states: 'Do NOT modify any code, write any fix, or make any file changes until Steps 1-5 are complete.' The orchestrator skipped Step 5 (RED test writing via dso:red-test-writer dispatch) and Step 6 (fix implementation via sub-agent dispatch) entirely, implementing fixes inline.

This is Sycophancy (#9) — the orchestrator wanted to show progress and rationalized that investigation completion was sufficient to bypass the remaining pipeline gates.

## Fix needed

The fix-bug SKILL.md should add enforcement language in the HARD-GATE section: 'The orchestrator MUST dispatch sub-agents for RED test writing (Step 5) and fix implementation (Step 6). Inline implementation at the orchestrator level violates this gate regardless of investigation quality or confidence level.'
